recalls.Description:
AliExpress has issued a recall for the 4-in-1 Baby Safety Cart Carriage, which can be converted into a hand-held infant carrier. This stroller was available in various colors, including pink, light pink, blue, dark blue, green, red, teal, gray, and black, and features a gray or black infant insert. The larger wheels are accented with yellow, but the product lacks any identifying markings.

The stroller's restraint system does not meet safety standards, which could lead to serious injuries or fatal falls. Consumers are urged to stop using this product immediately and reach out to AliExpress for a full refund. To process the refund, consumers must cut the restraints and send a photo of the destroyed stroller to the designated email address.
recalls.Title: AliExpress Recalls 4-in-1 Baby Safety Cart Carriage Due to Fall Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact AliExpress via email at us_product_recall@aliexpress.com or visit the AliExpress Buyer Help Center at www.aliexpress.com and click on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled 4-in-1 Baby Stroller - Black

Retailers.Name: Available online at AliExpress.com from September 2024 through October 2025, priced between $215 and $415.

Distributors.Name: Yanggu Caroline Electronic Commerce Co., Ltd. dba SX-BB Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to potential failure of the restraint system

Remedies.Name: Stop using the stroller and contact AliExpress for a full refund; cut the restraints and email a photo of the destroyed product.

recalls.metaDescription: AliExpress recalls the 4-in-1 Baby Safety Cart Carriage due to a serious fall hazard. Stop using it and seek a refund now.

recalls.Description: Olympia Tools has issued a recall for the Pack-N-Stroll Premium Folding Utility Wagon due to serious safety concerns. This utility wagon, featuring a black design with blue accents, includes a removable organizational tray and two cup holders on the push/pull handle. The wagon is foldable for convenient storage and is marked with 'Pack-N-Stroll' in blue lettering on a black label.
recalls.Title: Olympia Tools Recalls Pack-N-Stroll Utility Wagons Due to Entrapment and Fall Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can contact Olympia Tools at 877-461-8665 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email info@olympiatools.com, or visit www.cohooutdoors.com and select 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pack-N-Stroll Premium Folding Utility Wagon

Retailers.Name: Available at Costco.com and cohooutdoors.com from June 2025 to July 2025, priced between $200 and $400.

Importers.Name: Olympia Tools International, Inc. of Covina, California

Hazards.Name: The wagon's design violates stroller safety standards, as a gap between the organizational tray and sidewall can entrap a child's head, leading to serious injury or death. Additionally, the absence of a restraint system increases the risk of falls for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to immediately stop using the utility wagons and return them to Costco for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Olympia Tools recalls Pack-N-Stroll Utility Wagons due to serious safety hazards. Learn how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: AMTRADING has issued a recall for its black Cooling Gel Insert Pads designed for strollers and car seats. These pads feature an inner cooling layer that activates under the weight of a child. The Hoovy logo is displayed in white on the bottom right corner, and the dimensions of the pads are approximately 16.5 inches by 14.25 inches. Users should be aware that these pads can heat up if exposed to direct sunlight or left in a hot vehicle, creating a potential burn hazard for young children.
recalls.Title: AMTRADING Recalls Hoovy Cooling Gel Insert Pads for Car Seats and Strollers Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact AMTRADING at 888-259-1411 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email info@hoovyproducts.com. Additional information can be found at https://hoovyproducts.com by clicking on "Recall" or directly visiting https://hoovyproducts.com/pages/product-recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled Hoovy Cooling Gel Insert Pads shown in stroller
Recalled Hoovy Cooling Gel Insert Pads

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one consumer report where the child experienced second degree burns to his back, requiring medical attention.

Retailers.Name: Available at Target stores nationwide and online at Target.com and Walmart.com from March 2025 to July 2025, priced between $20 and $25.

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to overheating in sunlight or hot vehicles

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled Cooling Gel Insert Pad and contact AMTRADING for a full refund. Instructions for registration and disposal can be found at https://hoovyproducts.com/pages/product-recall.

recalls.metaDescription: AMTRADING recalls Hoovy Cooling Gel Insert Pads for car seats and strollers due to burn hazards. Learn how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: Fisher-Price has issued a recall for the Brunch & Go Stroller Toy due to a potential choking hazard. This item, identified by model number HGB85, features a pretend avocado toast along with three attachments: a mirror tomato, crinkle bacon, and an egg teether. The model number can be found on the tag attached to the toast.
recalls.Title: Fisher-Price Recalls Brunch & Go Stroller Toy Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled stroller toy and keep it away from children. For assistance, contact Fisher-Price at 855-853-6224 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx for more details.

Products.Name: Brunch & Go Stroller Toy
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 253,000 (In addition, about 4,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Brunch & Go Stroller Toy
Recalled Brunch & Go Stroller Toy
Recalled Brunch & Go Stroller Toy Tag with model number

Injuries.Name: Fisher-Price has received five reports of the yolk of the toy egg cracking and breaking. In two reports, an infant had a small piece in their mouth. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Hobby Lobby, Kohl's, Macy's, Marshalls, Nordstrom, Ross, TJMaxx, and Walmart stores nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com from February 2022 through March 2025 for approximately $13.

Distributors.Name: Fisher-Price Inc., of East Aurora, New York

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to small parts from cracked toy egg yolk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy, mark the egg with 'Recall', and contact Fisher-Price for a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Fisher-Price recalls Brunch & Go Stroller Toy due to choking hazard. Learn how to get a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Luv n' care has announced a recall of Nuby Stroller Fans, which feature three speed settings, adjustable tripod legs, and a rechargeable USB port designed for infants and toddlers. These fans, identifiable by their black plastic design and the Nuby logo on the front, have flexible legs that can wrap around stroller bars. The model number 25138 and lot number N8K10X can be found on a white label located on the back of the fan.
recalls.Title: Luv n' care Issues Recall for Nuby Stroller Fans Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Luv n' care at 800-588-6227 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email customers@nuby.com, or visit https://us.nuby.com/pages/fanrecall or www.nuby.us.com and navigate to Important Safety Information for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Nuby Stroller Fan (model and lot numbers on back of fan)
Recalled Nuby Stroller Fan (front)
Recalled Nuby Stroller Fan (side)
Recalled Nuby Stroller Fan (top)

Injuries.Name: Luv n' care has received seven reports of children's fingers accessing the fan blade, resulting in six laceration injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Baby Express, Burlington Coat Factory, Target, Unique Photo stores nationwide, and online at Amazon.com and us.nuby.com from May 2024 through June 2024 for approximately $15.

Importers.Name: Luv n' care, of Monroe, Louisiana

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration injury due to fan blade exposure

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the stroller fan immediately and contact Luv n' care for instructions on returning the product for a free replacement. The company is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Luv n' care recalls Nuby Stroller Fans due to a laceration risk. Contact for a free replacement and safety details.

recalls.Description: Stokke has issued a recall for YOYO³ strollers with serial numbers beginning with '142AA' and ending below '0062239.' Only these specific strollers are affected. Designed for children aged 0 to 48 months, these compact, foldable strollers come in various colors including black, Bonpoint beige, stone, olive, taupe, toffee, ginger, Air France blue, and aqua. The stroller features a black or black and white frame with wheels that have a white reflective center. The serial number can be found on a tracking label located beneath the stroller seat.
recalls.Title: Stokke Recalls YOYO³ Strollers Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Stokke at 877-978-6553 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. Monday through Friday, email info-usa@stokke.com, or visit https://recall.stokke.com/yoyo3 or www.stokke.com and navigate to 'Product Safety and Recall Information' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled YOYO³ Stroller (olive)
Location of Tracking Label with Serial Number on Recalled YOYO³ Stroller

Retailers.Name: Available in-store and online at Bloomingdale's, Crate and Barrel, Nordstrom, Albee Baby, and Macro Baby, as well as online at stokke.com, Amazon.com, and Babylist.com from September 2024 through October 2024, priced around $450.

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to potential parking brake disengagement

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled strollers immediately and visit https://recall.stokke.com/yoyo3 to register for a full refund via check.

recalls.metaDescription: Stokke recalls YOYO³ strollers due to a safety risk. Check if your stroller is affected and learn how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: Guava Family is recalling the Roam First Generation three-wheel foldable strollers produced from July 2020 to December 2021. These strollers feature a black frame with the 'Guava' logo in gray, and their dimensions are approximately 39 inches in length, 25 inches in width, and 43 inches in height, weighing around 28.5 pounds. The affected strollers can be identified by a label on the cross bar below the seat that includes 'Guava Family Inc.' along with the model name Roam and the manufacturing date. Only those manufactured within the specified date range are part of this recall.
recalls.Title: Guava Family Issues Recall for Roam Strollers Due to Fall Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled strollers and reach out to Guava Family Inc. at 833-408-0493 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at rs@realtimeresults.net. Additional details can be found at https://www.guavafamily.com under the 'Product Recall' section or directly at https://www.guavafamily.com/pages/stroller-recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled Guava Roam Stroller
Recalled strollers tracking label location on the cross bar below seat

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 67 reports of the brake lock disengaging. The firm has also received 152 reports of the brake pedal not engaging when pushed down. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.guavafamily.com and www.babylist.com from August 2020 through October 2023, priced between $500 and $550.

Importers.Name: Guava Family Inc., of Solana Beach, California

Hazards.Name: Brake failure leading to fall and injury risks for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Guava Family to obtain a free repair kit and updated installation instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Guava Family recalls Roam strollers due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free repair kit.

recalls.Description:
Stiger has announced a recall of the Besrey Twins Strollers, Model BR-C708S, due to serious safety concerns. These strollers feature gray and black fabric, black frames, and canopies that can be positioned up or down. Some units have a front grab bar covered in black fabric with 'besrey' printed in white, while others have a light gray fabric covering. The model number, manufacturing date, and 'STIGER, INC.' can be found on a label above the back right wheel. The instructional literature indicates the model number as BR-708S.

The strollers present multiple hazards, including entrapment risks in the front seat when facing forward, potential falls due to failures in the restraint system and rear seat enclosure, and a choking hazard from foam padding on the grab bar in black units. Consumers are urged to stop using the strollers immediately and reach out to Stiger for disposal instructions and a full refund.
recalls.Title: Stiger Recalls Besrey Twins Strollers Due to Safety Hazards and Federal Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Besrey at 888-310-6255 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or email support@besrey.com or stigerstg@outlook.com. More information is available at https://www.besrey.com/pages/product-recalls-safety-alerts or by visiting www.besrey.com and clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Besrey Twins Stroller in Gray and Black, Side View with Canopies Down
Recalled Besrey Twins Stroller in Gray, Side View with Canopies Down
Recalled Besrey Twins Stroller in Gray and Black, Side View with Canopies Up
Recalled Besrey Twins Stroller in Gray and Black, Front View
Recalled Besrey Twins Stroller in Gray, Front View
Recalled Besrey Twins Stroller, Label Location
Recalled Besrey Twins Stroller, Label Location
Recalled Besrey Twins Stroller, Label With Date of Manufacture and Model Number

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from July 2018 to September 2023 for approximately $200

Importers.Name: Stiger Inc., of Diamond Bar, California

Hazards.Name: Entrapment, fall, and choking hazards due to safety regulation violations

Remedies.Name: Stop using the stroller and contact Stiger for disposal instructions and a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: Stiger recalls Besrey Twins Strollers due to serious safety hazards. Stop using them and contact for a full refund.

recalls.Description: Bugaboo is recalling the seat component of the Dragonfly Seat Stroller due to a potential injury hazard. In parent-facing mode, the seat's backrest may move downward, which could result in an unrestrained infant falling from the seat. The affected seats measure approximately 11.8 inches wide and 22.8 inches long, available in black, gray, or forest green. Only seats with specific serial numbers are part of this recall.
recalls.Title: Bugaboo Recalls Dragonfly Seat Stroller Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bugaboo at 800-460-2922 from Monday to Thursday, 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, and Friday, 7 a.m. to 1 p.m. PT. You can also email service.us@bugaboo.com or visit www.bugaboo.com and click on 'Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: Bugaboo Dragonfly Seat Stroller
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,000 (In addition, about 200 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Dragonfly Seat Stroller

Injuries.Name: Bugaboo has received one report in the United States and seven reports in South Korea of the backrest of the seat moving downward during use when in parent-facing mode. No injuries have been reported in the U.S.

Retailers.Name: Available at children's specialty stores across the country and online at www.bugaboo.com from May 2023 to June 2023, priced around $900 for seat strollers and about $1,100 for bassinet strollers.

Importers.Name: Bugaboo North America Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of infant falling due to backrest movement in parent-facing mode

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled seats and contact Bugaboo for a free replacement

recalls.metaDescription: Bugaboo recalls Dragonfly Seat Stroller due to injury risk. Check if your seat is affected and learn how to get a free replacement.
